title: 1978 Asian Games

infobox.value: [Bhumibol Adulyadej](/wiki/Bhumibol_Adulyadej "Bhumibol Adulyadej") [King of Thailand](/wiki/King_of_Thailand "King of Thailand")

([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): กีฬาเอเชียนเกมส์ครั้งที่ 8), also known as **Bangkok 1978** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): กรุงเทพมหานคร 1978), were held from 9 to 20 December 1978, in [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok"), Thailand. Originally, the host city was [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore") but Singapore dropped its plan to host the Games due to financial problems. Then [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad"), the capital of Pakistan, was decided to host the 8th Games. But [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad") also dropped its plan to host the [Asian Games](/wiki/Asian_Games "Asian Games") due to conflicts with [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh") and India. The [Imperial State of Iran](/wiki/Pahlavi_Iran "Pahlavi Iran") withdrew because of the [Iranian revolution](/wiki/Iranian_revolution "Iranian revolution").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Multi-sport event in Bangkok, Thailand

VIII Asian Games

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Host city | [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok"), Thailand |
| Nations | 25 |
| Athletes | 3,842 |
| Events | 199 in 19 sports |
| Opening | 9 December 1978 |
| Closing | 20 December 1978 |
| Opened by | [Bhumibol Adulyadej](/wiki/Bhumibol_Adulyadej "Bhumibol Adulyadej") [King of Thailand](/wiki/King_of_Thailand "King of Thailand") |
| Athlete's Oath | [Preeda Chullamondhol](/wiki/Preeda_Chullamondhol "Preeda Chullamondhol")[[1]](#cite_note-tst-1) |
| Torch lighter | [Panomkorn Pladchurnil](/wiki/Panomkorn_Pladchurnil "Panomkorn Pladchurnil")[[1]](#cite_note-tst-1) [Ratchaneewan Bulakul](/wiki/Ratchaneewan_Bulakul "Ratchaneewan Bulakul") |
| Main venue | [National Stadium](/wiki/National_Stadium_(Thailand) "National Stadium (Thailand)") |
| Website | [ocasia.org (archived)](https://web.archive.org/web/20120620003321/http://ocasia.org/Game/GameParticular.aspx?9QoyD9QEWPe5ikIBubFL4g==) |
| ← [Tehran 1974](/wiki/1974_Asian_Games "1974 Asian Games")  [Delhi 1982](/wiki/1982_Asian_Games "1982 Asian Games") → | |

The **8th [Asian Games](/wiki/Asian_Games "Asian Games")** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): กีฬาเอเชียนเกมส์ครั้งที่ 8), also known as **Bangkok 1978** ([Thai](/wiki/Thai_language "Thai language"): กรุงเทพมหานคร 1978), were held from 9 to 20 December 1978, in [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok"), Thailand. Originally, the host city was [Singapore](/wiki/Singapore "Singapore") but Singapore dropped its plan to host the Games due to financial problems. Then [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad"), the capital of Pakistan, was decided to host the 8th Games. But [Islamabad](/wiki/Islamabad "Islamabad") also dropped its plan to host the [Asian Games](/wiki/Asian_Games "Asian Games") due to conflicts with [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh") and India. The [Imperial State of Iran](/wiki/Pahlavi_Iran "Pahlavi Iran") withdrew because of the [Iranian revolution](/wiki/Iranian_revolution "Iranian revolution").

[Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand") offered to help and the Asiad therefore was held in [Bangkok](/wiki/Bangkok "Bangkok"). On the political front, [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") was expelled from the Asian Games. A total number of 3,842 athletes, coming from 25 countries, competed in these Asian Games. Debuting sports were [archery](/wiki/Archery "Archery") and [bowling](/wiki/Bowling "Bowling").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Participating nations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1978_Asian_Games&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Participating nations")]

25 out of 32 [Olympic Council of Asia](/wiki/Olympic_Council_of_Asia "Olympic Council of Asia") members participated in these games. [Iran](/wiki/Iran "Iran") sent only one official and did not participate in the games due to the [political situation](/wiki/Iranian_Revolution "Iranian Revolution") in Iran at the time. [Democratic Kampuchea](/wiki/Democratic_Kampuchea "Democratic Kampuchea") also did not send any athletes and were represented by two officials.[[1]](#cite_note-tst-1)
